aweme_info.desc: 🇨🇳 Wulong Karst is a spectacular karst landscape in Chongqing, China, recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Famous for its Three Natural Bridges (the three largest natural stone bridges in Asia), Furong Cave, and deep ravines, this location offers a rare geological landscape and is often used as a filming location for famous films. ~ Here are some key points about Wulong Karst: 1. Location: Wulong District, Chongqing City, southwest China. 2. Key Features: Divided into three main areas, including the Three Natural Bridges (Tiansheng Sanqiao Bridge), Furong Cave, and Qingkou Tiankeng (sinkhole). UNESCO Status: Recognized as a World Natural Heritage Site in 2007 as part of the South China Karst. 3. Geological Features: This landscape was formed by the dissolution of limestone over millions of years, resulting in dramatic scenery, deep ravines, and unique natural stone bridges. 4. Popular Media: Filming location for famous films such as Transformers: Age of Extinction and Curse of the Golden Flower. Wulong Karst is a prime tourist destination for those seeking natural wonders and geological beauty. 🇨🇳 #wulongkarst #chongqing #china #fairyland #donghua

original_client_text.markup_text:
🇨🇳 Wulong Karst is a spectacular karst landscape in Chongqing, China, recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Famous for its Three Natural Bridges (the three largest natural stone bridges in Asia), Furong Cave, and deep ravines, this location offers a rare geological landscape and is often used as a filming location for famous films.
~ Here are some key points about Wulong Karst:
1. Location: Wulong District, Chongqing City, southwest China.
2. Key Features: Divided into three main areas, including the Three Natural Bridges (Tiansheng Sanqiao Bridge), Furong Cave, and Qingkou Tiankeng (sinkhole).
UNESCO Status: Recognized as a World Natural Heritage Site in 2007 as part of the South China Karst.
3. Geological Features: This landscape was formed by the dissolution of limestone over millions of years, resulting in dramatic scenery, deep ravines, and unique natural stone bridges.
4. Popular Media: Filming location for famous films such as Transformers: Age of Extinction and Curse of the Golden Flower.
Wulong Karst is a prime tourist destination for those seeking natural wonders and geological beauty. 🇨🇳
#wulongkarst #chongqing #china #fairyland #donghua

Location: Wulong District, Chongqing City, southwest China. 2. Key Features: Divided into three main areas, including the Three Natural Bridges (Tiansheng Sanqiao Bridge), Furong Cave, and Qingkou Tiankeng (sinkhole). UNESCO Status: Recognized as a World Natural Heritage Site in 2007 as part of the South China Karst. 3. Geological Features: This landscape was formed by the dissolution of limestone over millions of years, resulting in dramatic scenery, deep ravines, and unique natural stone bridges. 4. Popular Media: Filming location for famous films such as Transformers: Age of Extinction and Curse of the Golden Flower. Wulong Karst is a prime tourist destination for those seeking natural wonders and geological beauty. 🇨🇳 #wulongkarst #chongqing #china #fairyland #donghua

aweme_info.content_desc: ["For decades, the mine and refinery at Mountain Pass dominated the rare earth industry. But then mining, processing, and magnet-making began moving to China.","","The mine shuttered at the turn of the millennium. A decade later, a new company tried to revive the business, but failed and filed for bankruptcy in 2015.","","James Litinsky was running a hedge fund when he saw an opportunity and set out to restore the mine.","#rareearth #hedgefund #supplychain #criticalminerals "]
aweme_info.desc: For decades, the mine and refinery at Mountain Pass dominated the rare earth industry. But then mining, processing, and magnet-making began moving to China. The mine shuttered at the turn of the millennium. A decade later, a new company tried to revive the business, but failed and filed for bankruptcy in 2015. James Litinsky was running a hedge fund when he saw an opportunity and set out to restore the mine. #rareearth #hedgefund #supplychain #criticalminerals

original_client_text.markup_text:
For decades, the mine and refinery at Mountain Pass dominated the rare earth industry. But then mining, processing, and magnet-making began moving to China.

The mine shuttered at the turn of the millennium. A decade later, a new company tried to revive the business, but failed and filed for bankruptcy in 2015.

James Litinsky was running a hedge fund when he saw an opportunity and set out to restore the mine.
#rareearth #hedgefund #supplychain #criticalminerals

aweme_info.content_desc: ["“If you have a cell phone, have a laptop, if you drive a car, then you're touching rare earth minerals and rare earth magnets. It's essential to everyday life, but it's also essential to aerospace, telecom, defense systems,” says Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum.","","According to the military, one F-35 fighter jet contains about 100 pounds of rare earths.","","But the U.S. lacks a secure supply chain of these vital elements. China currently dominates the market. ","#rareearth #supplychain #uschina #nationalsecurity "]
aweme_info.desc: “If you have a cell phone, have a laptop, if you drive a car, then you're touching rare earth minerals and rare earth magnets. It's essential to everyday life, but it's also essential to aerospace, telecom, defense systems,” says Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum. According to the military, one F-35 fighter jet contains about 100 pounds of rare earths. But the U.S. lacks a secure supply chain of these vital elements. China currently dominates the market.  #rareearth #supplychain #uschina #nationalsecurity

allow_create_sticker.disable_toast: Create sticker isn’t available for this video

original_client_text.markup_text:
“If you have a cell phone, have a laptop, if you drive a car, then you're touching rare earth minerals and rare earth magnets. It's essential to everyday life, but it's also essential to aerospace, telecom, defense systems,” says Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum.

According to the military, one F-35 fighter jet contains about 100 pounds of rare earths.

But the U.S. lacks a secure supply chain of these vital elements. China currently dominates the market.
#rareearth #supplychain #uschina #nationalsecurity

aweme_info.content_desc: ["Experts say the key to securing critical rare earth minerals may not lie simply in opening new mines, but also in reusing what has already been extracted.","","If scaled successfully, rare earth recycling could strengthen supply chain security and lessen dependence on foreign sources, all while transforming how critical materials are sourced in the modern economy.","","As Professor Julie Klinger put it: \"It literally closes the loop.\"","#recycling #rareearthminerals #rareearths "]
aweme_info.desc: Experts say the key to securing critical rare earth minerals may not lie simply in opening new mines, but also in reusing what has already been extracted. If scaled successfully, rare earth recycling could strengthen supply chain security and lessen dependence on foreign sources, all while transforming how critical materials are sourced in the modern economy. As Professor Julie Klinger put it: "It literally closes the loop." #recycling #rareearthminerals #rareearths

Experts say the key to securing critical rare earth minerals may not lie simply in opening new mines, but also in reusing what has already been extracted.

If scaled successfully, rare earth recycling could strengthen supply chain security and lessen dependence on foreign sources, all while transforming how critical materials are sourced in the modern economy.

As Professor Julie Klinger put it: "It literally closes the loop."
#recycling #rareearthminerals #rareearths

aweme_info.content_desc: ["Michael Rosenthal and James Litinsky are the leaders of MP Materials, which runs the only active rare earth metals mine in the U.S. When they bought the mine, neither had any background in geology, but both saw the need to rebuild a domestic supply chain for these strategic metals.","","“The idea that this entire supply chain was on the other side of the world in China. It just occurred to us that someone had to help fix this problem,” says Litinsky.","","China dominates the rare earth market. These elements are essential to modern life, from smartphones and robotics to EVs, fighter jets, drones, and radar technology. ","#rareearth #criticalminerals #supplychain #mining #energysecurity "]
aweme_info.desc: Michael Rosenthal and James Litinsky are the leaders of MP Materials, which runs the only active rare earth metals mine in the U.S. When they bought the mine, neither had any background in geology, but both saw the need to rebuild a domestic supply chain for these strategic metals. “The idea that this entire supply chain was on the other side of the world in China. It just occurred to us that someone had to help fix this problem,” says Litinsky. China dominates the rare earth market. These elements are essential to modern life, from smartphones and robotics to EVs, fighter jets, drones, and radar technology.  #rareearth #criticalminerals #supplychain #mining #energysecurity

allow_create_sticker.disable_toast: Create sticker isn’t available for this video

original_client_text.markup_text:
Michael Rosenthal and James Litinsky are the leaders of MP Materials, which runs the only active rare earth metals mine in the U.S. When they bought the mine, neither had any background in geology, but both saw the need to rebuild a domestic supply chain for these strategic metals.

“The idea that this entire supply chain was on the other side of the world in China. It just occurred to us that someone had to help fix this problem,” says Litinsky.

China dominates the rare earth market. These elements are essential to modern life, from smartphones and robotics to EVs, fighter jets, drones, and radar technology.
#rareearth #criticalminerals #supplychain #mining #energysecurity

address_info.address: Rare Earth Mining Company Mine, Colorado 81243, USA

It just occurred to us that someone had to help fix this problem,” says Litinsky. China dominates the rare earth market. These elements are essential to modern life, from smartphones and robotics to EVs, fighter jets, drones, and radar technology.  #rareearth #criticalminerals #supplychain #mining #energysecurity

aweme_info.content_desc: ["More than 90% of the world’s rare earth magnets are made in China, says James Litinsky, chairman and CEO of MP Materials. That concentration gives China significant leverage over a critical supply chain.","","The public-private partnership with MP Materials aims to reinvigorate the U.S. rare earths industry - and bring the supply chain back to American shores.","","“Free markets work, but they don't work if you have an adversary that controls a monopoly that controls the prices,” says Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum. “To get this industry started again, we have to do some things to kick-start the private capital.”","#rareearthmetals #supplychain #nationalsecurity #usmanufacturing "]
aweme_info.desc: More than 90% of the world’s rare earth magnets are made in China, says James Litinsky, chairman and CEO of MP Materials. That concentration gives China significant leverage over a critical supply chain. The public-private partnership with MP Materials aims to reinvigorate the U.S. rare earths industry - and bring the supply chain back to American shores. “Free markets work, but they don't work if you have an adversary that controls a monopoly that controls the prices,” says Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum. “To get this industry started again, we have to do some things to kick-start the private capital.” #rareearthmetals #supplychain #nationalsecurity #usmanufacturing

allow_create_sticker.disable_toast: Create sticker isn’t available for this video

original_client_text.markup_text:
More than 90% of the world’s rare earth magnets are made in China, says James Litinsky, chairman and CEO of MP Materials. That concentration gives China significant leverage over a critical supply chain.

The public-private partnership with MP Materials aims to reinvigorate the U.S. rare earths industry - and bring the supply chain back to American shores.

“Free markets work, but they don't work if you have an adversary that controls a monopoly that controls the prices,” says Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum. “To get this industry started again, we have to do some things to kick-start the private capital.”
#rareearthmetals #supplychain #nationalsecurity #usmanufacturing

aweme_info.search_desc: ...rare earth magnets are made in China, says James Litinsky, chairman and CEO of MP Materials. That concentration gives China significant leverage over a critical supply chain. The public-private partnership with MP Materials aims to reinvigorate the U.S. rare earths industry - and bring the supply chain back to American shores. “Free markets work, but they don't work if you have an adversary that controls a monopoly that controls the prices,” says Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum. “To get this industry started again, we have to do some things to kick-start the private capital.” #rareearthmetals #supplychain #nationalsecurity #usmanufacturing

aweme_info.content_desc: ["Golden silk jade is a rare variety of nephrite jade found exclusively in the regions along China's ancient Silk Road, particularly in areas of Xinjiang and Gansu province where the geological conditions produce its distinctive golden yellow coloration. The color comes from iron oxide deposits within the stone that develop over millions of years, and the degree of translucency in high quality specimens is what separates premium pieces from lower grade material. In Chinese culture jade has been considered the most precious of all stones for thousands of years, with golden silk jade specifically associated with wealth, prosperity, and protection. The combination of rarity, cultural significance, and visual quality makes it one of the most sought after collector stones in the Chinese luxury market.","","#nature #geology #china #luxury #minerals"]
aweme_info.desc: Golden silk jade is a rare variety of nephrite jade found exclusively in the regions along China's ancient Silk Road, particularly in areas of Xinjiang and Gansu province where the geological conditions produce its distinctive golden yellow coloration. The color comes from iron oxide deposits within the stone that develop over millions of years, and the degree of translucency in high quality specimens is what separates premium pieces from lower grade material. In Chinese culture jade has been considered the most precious of all stones for thousands of years, with golden silk jade specifically associated with wealth, prosperity, and protection. The combination of rarity, cultural significance, and visual quality makes it one of the most sought after collector stones in the Chinese luxury market. #nature #geology #china #luxury #minerals

original_client_text.markup_text:
Golden silk jade is a rare variety of nephrite jade found exclusively in the regions along China's ancient Silk Road, particularly in areas of Xinjiang and Gansu province where the geological conditions produce its distinctive golden yellow coloration. The color comes from iron oxide deposits within the stone that develop over millions of years, and the degree of translucency in high quality specimens is what separates premium pieces from lower grade material. In Chinese culture jade has been considered the most precious of all stones for thousands of years, with golden silk jade specifically associated with wealth, prosperity, and protection. The combination of rarity, cultural significance, and visual quality makes it one of the most sought after collector stones in the Chinese luxury market.

#nature #geology #china #luxury #minerals

aweme_info.search_desc: Golden silk jade is a rare variety of nephrite jade found exclusively in the regions along China's ancient Silk Road, particularly in areas of Xinjiang and Gansu province where the geological conditions produce its distinctive golden yellow coloration.

aweme_info.desc: Helium-3 ( ￼ ￼ ) is a rare, non-radioactive isotope with immense potential as a clean nuclear fusion fuel. When fused with deuterium, it produces massive, charged energy with minimal radiation or radioactive waste. Though scarce on Earth, vast quantities exist in lunar soil, driving interest in mining the Moon for future, high-efficiency energy production.  ￼YouTube +3 Key Aspects of Helium-3 Energy The Fusion Reaction:  ￼ ￼  fusion, particularly with Deuterium (D- ￼ ￼ ), produces charged particles rather than neutrons. These charged particles can be guided by magnetic fields, allowing for direct conversion to electricity (potentially over 80% efficiency) without large-scale steam turbines. Benefits: Minimal Radiation: Nearly aneutronic (produces few to no neutrons), which minimizes radioactive waste and structural damage to reactors. Clean Energy: No long-lived radioactive waste, reducing the environmental footprint. High Energy Density: The energy yield is massive, with potential to power global needs for centuries. Challenges: Scarcity on Earth:  ￼ ￼  is exceptionally rare on Earth, primarily available in small quantities from nuclear warhead tritium decay. High Temperature Requirements: Fusing  ￼ ￼  requires higher temperatures (often in the billions of degrees) than conventional Deuterium-Tritium (D-T) fusion. Mining Costs: Retrieving  ￼ ￼  from the Moon (deposited by solar winds) and transporting it to Earth involves enormous technological and economic challenges.  ￼YouTube +8 Helium-3 as a Future Energy Source Lunar Mining: The Moon is estimated to have over 1 million metric tons of  ￼ ￼  in its regolith, potentially enough to satisfy global energy demand for thousands of years. Private Interest: Companies like Helion Energy are developing fusion reactors that utilize deuterium and helium-3, aiming for direct energy conversion. Development Phase: While promising, practical  ￼ ￼  fusion reactors are still in the developmental phase, with estimates for commercial viability often placed toward the mid-to-late 21st century.  ￼ResearchGate +4 Current Applications Neutron Detectors: Used in homeland security at ports and airports to detect radioactive materials. Quantum Computing: Crucial as a cooling agent in cryogenic equipment, allowing elements to reach temperatures of  ￼ ￼ .

original_client_text.markup_text:
Helium-3 (

￼

￼

) is a rare, non-radioactive isotope with immense potential as a clean nuclear fusion fuel. When fused with deuterium, it produces massive, charged energy with minimal radiation or radioactive waste. Though scarce on Earth, vast quantities exist in lunar soil, driving interest in mining the Moon for future, high-efficiency energy production. 

￼YouTube +3

Key Aspects of Helium-3 Energy

The Fusion Reaction: 

￼

￼

 fusion, particularly with Deuterium (D-

￼

￼

), produces charged particles rather than neutrons. These charged particles can be guided by magnetic fields, allowing for direct conversion to electricity (potentially over 80% efficiency) without large-scale steam turbines.

Benefits:

Minimal Radiation: Nearly aneutronic (produces few to no neutrons), which minimizes radioactive waste and structural damage to reactors.

Clean Energy: No long-lived radioactive waste, reducing the environmental footprint.

High Energy Density: The energy yield is massive, with potential to power global needs for centuries.

Challenges:

Scarcity on Earth: 

￼

￼

 is exceptionally rare on Earth, primarily available in small quantities from nuclear warhead tritium decay.

High Temperature Requirements: Fusing 

￼

￼

 requires higher temperatures (often in the billions of degrees) than conventional Deuterium-Tritium (D-T) fusion.

Mining Costs: Retrieving 

￼

￼

 from the Moon (deposited by solar winds) and transporting it to Earth involves enormous technological and economic challenges. 

￼YouTube +8

Helium-3 as a Future Energy Source

Lunar Mining: The Moon is estimated to have over 1 million metric tons of 

￼

￼

 in its regolith, potentially enough to satisfy global energy demand for thousands of years.

Private Interest: Companies like Helion Energy are developing fusion reactors that utilize deuterium and helium-3, aiming for direct energy conversion.

Development Phase: While promising, practical 

￼

￼

 fusion reactors are still in the developmental phase, with estimates for commercial viability often placed toward the mid-to-late 21st century. 

￼ResearchGate +4

Current Applications

Neutron Detectors: Used in homeland security at ports and airports to detect radioactive materials.

Quantum Computing: Crucial as a cooling agent in cryogenic equipment, allowing elements to reach temperatures of 

￼

￼

.

aweme_info.content_desc: ["A critical mineral most people have never heard of just became a geopolitical flashpoint.","","China controls nearly half of global antimony supply.","","Exports to the U.S. dropped ~97%.","","Prices surged over 200%.","","Now one small U.S. company is at the center of defense supply chains.","","This is how global tensions quietly reshape industries, prices, and market narratives.","","Follow @foxofstocks to stay informed on what markets are starting to watch \ud83e\udd8a"]
aweme_info.desc: A critical mineral most people have never heard of just became a geopolitical flashpoint. China controls nearly half of global antimony supply. Exports to the U.S. dropped ~97%. Prices surged over 200%. Now one small U.S. company is at the center of defense supply chains. This is how global tensions quietly reshape industries, prices, and market narratives. Follow @foxofstocks to stay informed on what markets are starting to watch 🦊

aweme_info.content_desc: ["These rocks look like dragon scales \ud83d\ude33","","In Guizhou,","there’s a place called Dragon Scale Stones.","","Rocks stacked layer upon layer","pointing straight into the sky.","","Sharp.","Wild.","Almost unreal.","","From far away","it looks like a giant dragon’s back.","","But it’s completely natural.","","Formed over millions of years.","","Have you ever seen anything like this?","","#China #Guizhou #DragonScale #NatureMystery #AmazingNature "]
aweme_info.desc: These rocks look like dragon scales 😳 In Guizhou, there’s a place called Dragon Scale Stones. Rocks stacked layer upon layer pointing straight into the sky. Sharp. Wild. Almost unreal. From far away it looks like a giant dragon’s back. But it’s completely natural. Formed over millions of years. Have you ever seen anything like this? #China #Guizhou #DragonScale #NatureMystery #AmazingNature

A discoery in Wyoming thereatens to weaken The monopolistic stranglehold over the US, Japan, South Korea, and soon India witn a massive discovery in wyoming. #rareearth #rareearthmetals #commodity #wyoming #japan #southkorea #india #halleckcreek #epa #environment #neodymium #lithium #rareearths #elements
